Executive Summary

Account performance at a glance

The account belongs to Poly Furniture of Williamsburg, LLC, an Amish and poly furniture retailer in the Newport News/Williamsburg, Virginia area. With 3 active campaigns, an optimization score of 87.9%, and $7,650 spent generating 862 reported conversions at $8.88 cost per conversion, the surface numbers look favorable. However, the account has meaningful structural issues, and the conversion figures require important context about what is actually being measured.

⚠️

Important context on reported conversions

The 862 reported conversions are predominantly Google Business Profile micro-engagements — direction requests, map views, and website visits — not contact requests or in-store appointments. The $8.88 cost per conversion figure does not reflect true lead or sales efficiency. The website currently only offers an email button as a contact method, which means there is no tracked conversion event tied to genuine customer inquiries. All-time account spend is $31,787 with 4,131 total conversions at $7.69/conv, all subject to the same caveat.

Campaign Analysis

Three campaigns with notably different results

The account runs one Performance Max campaign and two Smart campaigns. Performance gaps between them are wide, and the campaign generating the most activity is currently paused.

🚨

Campaign 1 (highest activity) is PAUSED and conversions have stopped

The Spring 2026 PMax campaign accounts for 98% of all account conversions at $7.88/conv. It is currently paused, which means the account's primary source of activity is completely off. Unless there is a deliberate seasonal reason, this should be reviewed and addressed promptly. The account is currently relying entirely on two Smart campaigns with near-zero conversion efficiency.

Conversion Tracking

18 conversion actions: what is actually being counted

The account has 18 conversion actions across multiple categories. Most of the 862 reported conversions are passive micro-engagements from Google Business Profile interactions, not contact requests. The website offers only an email button as a contact path, meaning no tracked conversion event ties back to a genuine customer inquiry.

🔴

No tracked conversion for actual contact requests

The website currently provides only an email button as a way for customers to get in touch. Email button clicks are not being tracked as a conversion event. This means Google's Smart Bidding has no signal tied to genuine customer inquiries, and the account cannot measure how many ads actually resulted in a prospect reaching out.

💡

Recommended: add a contact form with a thank-you page

Replacing or supplementing the email button with a contact or quote request form, followed by a dedicated thank-you page, would allow a clean conversion event to be set up in GA4. Every form submission would register as a meaningful conversion, giving Smart Bidding a reliable signal and providing a true picture of cost per qualified inquiry.

Strengths ✅

What the account is doing well

Despite the structural issues, the account has several genuine positives that provide a solid foundation to build on.

✓

PMax Campaign Drives High Micro-Engagement Volume

The Spring 2026 PMax campaign generated 844 conversions at $7.88/conv with a 13% conversion rate. These figures reflect micro-engagement activity rather than verified leads, but the volume indicates the algorithm is successfully reaching a relevant local audience. The campaign infrastructure is working.

✓

Strong Sitelink Asset Coverage

AI-generated sitelinks (Dining Chair Styles, Gliders And Swings, Shop Our Accessories, Our Services, Daisy Collection) all show healthy CTRs of 8 to 17%, indicating well-aligned ad extensions that match user intent.

✓

Negative Keywords on PMax

22 negative keywords block competitor brands (Bob's Furniture, Grand Furniture) and irrelevant queries (cheap/used/thrift furniture, flea markets). Good competitive protection is already in place on the best-performing campaign.

✓

Optimization Score of 87.9%

The account shows a recent +5% improvement in optimization score, indicating active management attention and responsiveness to Google's recommendations over time.

✓

Multi-Format Campaign Mix

Running both Performance Max (AI-driven, multi-channel) and Smart Campaigns (local-focus) provides reach across placements and diversifies delivery mechanisms appropriate for a local retail business.

✓

Reasonable Budget Allocation

Total daily budget of roughly $114/day across active campaigns is appropriate for the market size and competitive landscape for specialty furniture in the Newport News/Williamsburg area.

Points Deserving Attention ⚠️

Issues requiring action

The account has several issues that are directly affecting spend efficiency or blocking the account's ability to measure and improve business outcomes.

Critical

🔴 Best campaign is PAUSED

The Spring 2026 PMax campaign is responsible for 98% of all conversions at $7.88/conv and is currently paused. Unless there is a deliberate seasonal rationale, this should be reviewed immediately.

  • 844 of 862 total conversions came from this campaign
  • Pause has halted virtually all account engagement activity
  • The remaining two campaigns cannot compensate at current settings
Critical

🔴 Campaign 2: 0.07% Conversion Rate (Likely Misconfigured Conversion)

The In-Stock Poly Furniture campaign shows only 2 conversions at $439.87 each. The conversion goal appears to be set to the "Special Deals" page view — a passive visit rather than a genuine customer action. This inflates CPA and misdirects Smart Bidding.

  • Conversion goal is likely a page view event, not a contact or inquiry action
  • The 0.07% rate likely reflects users not reaching that specific page, not a lead quality issue
  • Audit and correct the conversion goal before changing bid strategy
Critical

🔴 No Tracked Conversion for Customer Inquiries

The website only offers an email button, and email button clicks are not tracked as a conversion event. Smart Bidding has no signal tied to actual customer contact, making it unable to optimize toward the business outcomes that matter.

  • No form submission event tied to GA4 or Google Ads
  • No thank-you page to anchor a clean conversion signal
  • Reported CPA is based entirely on micro-engagements, not inquiries

Significant Issues

!

No Negative Keywords on Smart Campaigns

Only the PMax campaign has a negative keyword list. Both Smart campaigns could be triggering on competitor brands, discount searches, and irrelevant queries with no filtering in place.

!

No Target CPA Set (Google Recommendation)

Google recommends adding a Target CPA on the PMax campaign (+8% optimization score potential). Currently running Maximize Conversions without a spending cap creates risk when the campaign is reactivated.

!

PMax Asset Group Below "Excellent"

Google flagged asset groups in the PMax campaign as below "Excellent" strength, suggesting missing creative assets (headlines, descriptions, images, or video). This limits placement across all channels.

!

No Ad Scheduling (Campaigns Running 24/7)

All campaigns run at all hours every day. Without reviewing hour-of-day and day-of-week performance data, budget may be reaching low-intent windows. A review of performance by time segment would clarify whether adjustments are warranted.

!

Download Conversion Misconfigured

The "Download" conversion action shows "Misconfigured" status in account diagnostics, with 0 conversions recording. This needs to be audited, corrected, or removed to keep the account clean.

Recommended Improvements 🔧

What should be done, in priority order

Recommendations ranked from most to least impact on account performance. The first four actions address root causes that limit everything else the account can achieve.

01

Re-enable or Replace Spring 2026 PMax

If paused for a seasonal reason, establish a clear restart date. If paused in error, reactivate immediately. Consider running year-round with seasonal budget adjustments rather than full pauses.

📌

Expected impact of addressing the four critical issues

Reactivating the PMax campaign, adding a contact form with conversion tracking, and fixing Campaign 2's bid strategy would address the root causes behind most of the account's current inefficiencies. Once a form submission conversion event is in place, the account will have a genuine cost-per-inquiry metric for the first time, and Smart Bidding will have the signal it needs to optimize toward actual business outcomes rather than passive engagement activity.

📋

Key takeaway

The account's core challenge is not budget or ad quality — it is that the two most fundamental conditions for effective Google Ads are not in place: there is no conversion event tied to genuine customer contact. Adding a contact form with a thank-you page would give the account a real measurement foundation for the first time. From there, the PMax campaign's engagement signals suggest it can find the right audience in the local market once properly constrained.

Google Ads Account Audit  ·  Poly Furniture of Williamsburg, LLC  ·  Newport News / Williamsburg, VA
Report Date: May 26, 2026  ·  Review Period: Oct 5, 2023 to May 26, 2026  ·  All-Time Spend: $31,787  ·  All-Time Conversions: 4,131 at $7.69/conv
Data sourced directly from account dashboard. Conversion counts reflect Primary conversion actions. "Conversions" include micro-engagements from Google Business Profile (directions, website visits, engagement events) unless otherwise noted.
